headlines.title: Asia stocks mixed as tech losses, Iran fears weigh; Japan Q1 GDP beats forecasts
Dollar steady as bond rout stalls, Japanese yen weakens past strong GDP
Japan, South Korea set to agree on oil cooperation, Nikkei says
Japan's Nikkei surrenders early gain as tech shares fall
Asian shares trade mixed and Kospi falls nearly 4% as oil prices keep swinging
Japan to Triple International Tourist Departure Tax to 3,000 Yen per Trip
Japan Q1 GDP Beats Forecasts, BOJ June Hike in View

marketTone.ko: 오늘 일본 증시는 기술주 하락과 아시아 전반의 혼조세 속에서 초기 상승분을 반납하며 마감했습니다. 일본의 1분기 GDP가 예상을 상회하며 6월 일본은행(BOJ)의 금리 인상 기대감을 높였으나, 엔화는 오히려 약세를 보였습니다. 이란 관련 지정학적 우려와 유가 변동성 또한 시장 전반에 부담으로 작용했습니다.
marketTone.en: Japanese stocks saw a mixed performance today, with early gains reversed as tech shares declined, mirroring broader Asian market trends. Despite Japan's Q1 GDP beating forecasts, which fueled expectations for a potential Bank of Japan rate hike in June, the yen weakened. Geopolitical concerns, particularly regarding Iran, and fluctuating oil prices also contributed to a cautious sentiment across the region.
